pattern: Backtracking with a skyline profile: track the height of each column and always fill the lowest point with the largest square that fits, pruning with a best-so-far bound.
hints: The rectangle is small (at most 13x13), so a well-pruned backtracking search is feasible; the key is choosing which cell to fill next.
Maintain a column-height skyline; always pick the leftmost column with the minimum height and try placing squares of all valid sizes starting from the maximum.
Prune branches where the current square count already equals or exceeds the best found so far.

edge_inputs.description: small rectangle with clean square tiling
asymmetric: n and m swapped should give same answer

stress.description: random small n and m in 1..13
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 13)
    m = rng.randint(1, 13)
    return [n, m]
